reviews.review_msg: I hear she is ok in intro to marketing since there are less interaction with students in a bigger setting. However she is horrible in upper level marketing. She is not clear, she is bias and will put you down if she does not agree with you. She is very discouraging and are not clear with her marking. She is enthusiastic however very emotional .

It's surprising for me that Cathy has such a low rating. She is very intelligent and shows great passion in marketing. Lectures are interesting to attend. I learned a lot from her. 3 essays, 1 group presentation,  MT and Final (all MCQ's).
worst prof ever

what a painful class. NOTHING in class she says is of any relevance. ALWAYS end class late. USELESS assignments and UNCLEAR criterias. I really don't know why she's teaching, she won't even understand your questions and gives complete unrelated answers. She should be fired. Most unfair (simply random)marker I've ever had in a course. Avoid her!! :(

Lectures consisted of nothing but her dumb obnoxious life stories! don't bother emailing her b/c she responds with something else, screwed up the midterm it was missing papers and was in the wrong order!, gives NO grading rubrics for her assignments which are a complete waste of time and the list goes on. Horrible professor!

Simply horrible, unorganized. 3 pages on midterm was missing, screwed up our final mark and had to appeal to the dean. Ask her a question in email and she will reply something completely unrelated. EXTREMELY unfair marker. She gives a three-line question, expect a report, and write out a feedback that's 4 page(single spaced) with tons of typo.

unclear criteria, extremely subjective in marking, and favors certain students. 1st presentation - make the class ask questions & handle them appropriately, if your presentation is too good with no questions, she will just give you the average class mark. Interesting lecturer, but unfair marking scheme.
Cool prof :D, too bad she's not teaching 343 anymore.
super funny. class is fairly easy, she makes it very interesting. go to lectures
Her exams are all about memorization, shes a bit annoying but not awful.  She can be entertaining at times

Awesome personality, awesome subject knowledge and way of presenting it. You don't fall asleep as she adds so many great examples and provides explanations that are not as dry as book stuff. She's perfect for this course because otherwise, the material is mostly theories and definitions..
for those who bother to go to her lectures, she can be pretty interesting at times. Her tests are based from the text book, thus the minimal attendance level.

It is not a creative course, but definitely practical. Some people may not find her helpful because she doesn't give out answers easily. This is HOW YOU LEARN. Of course she knows the answers well enough, but the intention is that she wants you to know this is how the real world works. She will let you start from zero. =) have fun.

She is soooooo energetic. Course itself is too easy but everybody does so well, so you gotta study hard. This class's midterm average was 75% in this summer! what the hell! But Cathy is a good prof. She knows stuff so well and is always so energetic so you wont fall asleep in the lecture.

One of the best classes and best professors.  Actually prepared me for the business world; real world experience was gained, and Cathy is one of the most informative, entertaining and valuable professors at SFU!

I hated this class until two days after it was finished. The project is the most realistic experience short of co-op you'll get in SFU business. My mark was higher than I thought it would be. Fear politics, I tell you! If Cathy was a musician, she would likely be the Ace of Base
No Comments
Not worth the money you paid. Learn nothing.Can you imagine you feel bored in this class which should be interesting. Boring!!!
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments
Knows her stuff, very approachable, tries her best to be helpful. Competitive Grading though.
No Comments
No Comments

Blame yrself for not learning. Txtbk: useless but necessary, like a stepping stone. Learned the most while doing the proj. It wasn't really a chance to apply the material learned, it was more like an opportunity to learn while you're doing it. Group project in this course is the closest you'll get to working in real life; that alone is invaluable.

Her facetiousness is a nice change from the usual stoic academics we are forced to endure.  Cryptic at times with regards to expectations, but gives great direct feedback - unwaveringly honest but tempered with empathy.

I learned a lot in this class!  Although this was a tough course, but fun and interesting. It was different from any other course.  Be prepared to spend hours and hours in this course! For the project, you have to work closely with your team members (like a family..lol)  Besides lectures, most of the learning came from the interaction b/w members

The problem with this course was the lack of an accurate mechanism to match a student's group performance with her/his grade. Also, this 4th year course did not encourage vigorous research methods: no statistics, no SPSS, no measurement whatsoever. And this is a sales and promotions course, where numbers should matter.

Took 448 in Summer2005, and found project work and class environment to be valuable. Anyone can read a textbook, but a book can't provide you with what Cathy does (prompt feedback, support, etc).  Learning does not depend solely on the prof. Student below suggests that everybody didn't learn anything. I did, and I doubt I'm the only one.

What did I learn from her course? None. She repeated the textbook, no interesting examples, no pictures, no movies...nothing!! Why did someone say she is entertaining? Read the textbook by yourself, that is all! Her feedback is really little. if you ask anyone in my class, all of them will say they learned nothing.
Her standards are too high. Lectures are a WASTE(dont even bother to go). To do get an "A" memorize the damn freakin textbook and all the 18 chapters.
Disguises herself with her sense of humor, but short answer questions for final! That's 18 chapters!! TERRIBLE.
Talks too much but has a great sense of humour.  I had her as a TA and she wasn't that great but as an lecturer, she's good.  Exams are easy but the marking scheme is odd.
Awesome prof!! She's the best prof I've ever had at SFU.
Fun, enthusiastic, and educational!

She is so full of energy for the whole 4 hours of lecture. Her accent and great sense of humour will definitely keep you awake for the entire class. Just make sure you keep up with all the deadlines or she'll hang you to death!
